sourceMovie.title: Death Wish
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Death Wish for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Eli Roth keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Death Sentence
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Death Wish clicked for you, Death Sentence (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Death Wish clicked for you, The Equalizer (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Death Wish?
faq.answer: Try Death Sentence, The Equalizer, Jackie Brown—each shares DNA with Death Wish while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Death Wish?
faq.answer: Death Wish (2018) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Death Wish worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
